findings.message:
The following WebExtension API is not supported in the targeted Thunderbird version.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.

The following WebExtension API is not supported in the targeted Thunderbird version.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.
"messagesRead" permission is required here, but is not declared in the manifest. Add it to the requested permissions.

"messages.oldOne" is deprecated in the targeted Thunderbird version. Please migrate to a supported API.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.
The add-on is setting strict_min_version to 128.0 but uses functions or events which were added later. Please adjust the value of strict_min_version accordingly.

findings.hint: The legacy API was removed; use messages instead.
findings.message:
"legacy.doThing" is deprecated in the targeted Thunderbird version. Please migrate to a supported API.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.
"accountsRead" permission is required here, but is not declared in the manifest. Add it to the requested permissions.
"messagesMove" permission is required here, but is not declared in the manifest. Add it to the requested permissions.
action.onClicked needs a manifest key that is not declared: "action". Add one of those keys to manifest.json.
The add-on makes a synchronous XMLHttpRequest, which can lock up the user interface. Please use an asynchronous request instead.

Inserting markup via ".innerHTML" is not permitted. The only supported way to do so is the Sanitizer API (Element.setHTML(), Thunderbird 148+). Please also consider alternatives to (re-)building the live DOM (use textContent, a <template>, or CSS to show and hide existing elements).
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/en/mv3/guides/innerHTML.html.
Unconditional debugger statement. Please remove it or move it behind a config flag.

Asynchronous listener attached to runtime.onMessage. This is error-prone and must be avoided.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/en/mv3/guides/runtimeMessaging.html
Static analysis flagged a likely code error: no-redeclare: 'duplicate' is already defined.

Adding an add-on icon in manifest.json improves user acceptance.
Read more: https://developer.thunderbird.net/add-ons/mailextensions/supported-manifest-keys#icons

Invalid manifest.json: one or more requested permissions are unknown.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.

The manifest requests one or more very broad host permissions that are granted at install. Request only the specific hosts your add-on actually needs. If it genuinely needs broad access, declare them as optional host permissions so the user can grant them on demand rather than up front.
Read more: https://thunderbird.topicbox.com/groups/addons/Tafa58394231a18f8-M3e565a75287313ea4395ff5f/prefer-to-use-optionalhostpermissions-to-get-around-cors-issues-with-fetch
The following referenced file(s) are not bundled with the add-on, or the path may be wrongly specified. Please include every referenced file in the package, and check that the used paths are correct.

Invalid manifest.json: the following top-level keys are unknown and will be ignored by Thunderbird.
Read more: https://webextension-api.thunderbird.net/.

The following file(s) or folder(s) appear to be unused or hidden. Such files complicate the review, may leak information about the system the XPI was built on, or needlessly increase the size of the add-on. Please correct your packaging process so they are not included.
Resources are loaded from a remote source. Remote scripts and libraries are not allowed. Please bundle all resources with your add-on so they can be reviewed.

The following file(s) or folder(s) appear to be unused or hidden. Such files complicate the review, may leak information about the system the XPI was built on, or needlessly increase the size of the add-on. Please correct your packaging process so they are not included.
Resources are loaded from a remote source. Remote scripts and libraries are not allowed. Please bundle all resources with your add-on so they can be reviewed.
